data.title: Veteran-owned Dickey’s Barbecue Pit in Perrysburg, Ohio reopens after kitchen fire, franchise owner Steve Mowery excited to serve once more.Characters: 152
data.description: In Toledo, Ohio, Dickey's Barbecue Pit on Fremont Pike has reopened after a kitchen fire forced a shutdown. The franchise owner, US Army Combat Veteran Steve Mowery, has relaunched the beloved barbecue spot to serve the greater Toledo area. Mowery, who is also a local deputy sheriff, spent months reconstructing the restaurant. Known for its iconic pit-smoked dishes, the restaurant is ready to welcome back customers.

data.title: Listrak's 2026 Beauty & Fashion Benchmark Report highlights revenue impact of transactional messages for retailers.
data.description: In a recent report utilizing Listrak Intelligence, beauty and fashion brands can gain valuable insights for enhancing personalized cross-channel marketing strategies. Released on March 4, 2026 in Lititz, Pennsylvania, the report focuses on improving transactional and post-purchase communications for retailers in these sectors. This data-driven approach aims to optimize marketing efforts by delivering targeted messages to consumers.

data.title: Scripps to buy WTVQ in Lexington, Kentucky from Morris Network for $15.8 million.
data.description: E.W. Scripps Company recently finalized a deal to acquire WTVQ, a prominent ABC affiliate station located in Lexington, Kentucky. The purchase was made from Morris Network, Inc. for a total of $15.8 million.

data.title: Southminster partners for arts-integrated wellness in CCRCs. Launches "Creative Aging on Prescription" study.
data.description: In Charlotte, NC on March 4, 2026, Southminster revealed its recent partnership to establish itself as a pioneer in arts-integrated wellness among continuous care retirement communities (CCRCs) nationwide. By joining forces with Lifetime Arts and Wake Forest University, Southminster aims to enhance the well-being of its residents through innovative programs combining arts and health.
